candidates.row_label: Depreciation / amortization / write-down Cost of product sold
candidates.why: This is the only operating D&A row explicitly tied to the selected COGS bucket. However, it appears to be a component within the parent row 'Cost of products sold' rather than a separate expense to add: the nearby 'Cost of products sold - Balancing value' plus this D&A row and other adjustments reconcile to the selected COGS total, indicating inclusion in the already selected parent row.

variables.notes: No separate COGS D&A should be added. The disclosed COGS D&A appears embedded within the already selected parent total 'Cost of products sold', so adding it separately would risk double counting.

candidates.why: Explicit operating D&A tied to the selected parent row 'Selling expenses', but it appears to be a component inside that parent total rather than a separate add-on line. The presence of 'Selling expenses - Balancing value' and comparability items suggests reconciliation within the total.
Explicit operating D&A tied to the selected parent row 'Administrative expenses', but presented as a component of that already selected parent bucket rather than a separate same-level expense to add.

candidates.row_label: Amortization excluding Goodwill in Research & Development Expenses
candidates.why: This appears to be a component within the selected parent row 'R&D expenses'. Because 'R&D expenses' was already selected in both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, adding this R&D-related D&A separately would likely distort the intended PROF construction and risk double counting.
Also appears to be a component within 'R&D expenses'. Since 'R&D expenses' is already selected both in XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD, this should not be separately added under XSGA_DA.

variables.notes: No separate XSGA D&A should be added. The selling/admin/R&D D&A lines appear to be sub-components embedded in the already selected parent rows, and R&D is already handled symmetrically through both XSGA_COMPONENTS and XRD.
